data.title: Chevron (CVX) Competes for $22 Billion Lukoil Asset Sale as U.S. Deadline Nears
data.description:
Several major investors are racing to buy $22 billion of global oil assets owned by Russia's Lukoil, as a U.S. Treasury deadline nears. The leading bidders include private equity firm Carlyle Group Inc (CG), a group led by Chevron Corporation (CVX), and Abu Dhabi-based International Holding Company.
The sale is taking place in the midst of Western sanctions that prevent Lukoil from holding these assets following Russia's war in Ukraine. As a result, any deal must win approval from the U.S. Treasury before it can close.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The assets are spread across several regions and include oil fields in Iraq, Azerbaijan, and Kazakhstan. They also include refineries in Italy, the Netherlands, Bulgaria, and Romania, as well as more than 2,000 fuel stations across Europe and the U.S. Earlier this month, reports indicated that Chevron plans to team up with Quantum Capital Group and divide the assets after the purchase. At the same time, Carlyle is working with Goldman Sachs (GS) as it prepares its own bid.

Meanwhile, CVX shares rose 0.14% on Monday, closing at $162.34.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Citi Upgrades Palantir Stock (PLTR) to Buy, Sees Explosive 80% Revenue Growth
data.description:
Citi's four-star-rated analyst Tyler Radke has upgraded his rating on Palantir Technologies (PLTR) from Hold to Buy, citing an expected explosive 80% revenue growth in 2026. Meanwhile, Radke raised his price target from $190 to $235, predicting an upside of over 30%. He also highlighted the upcoming Q4 results as a key catalyst, signaling growing confidence in the company's AI-driven data analytics business.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Citi Turned Bullish on Palantir

Radke pointed to growing enterprise AI spending and rising government modernization budgets as the main reasons for turning bullish on Palantir.

According to Radke, Palantir's enterprise use cases are gaining momentum, based on its recent CIO survey and industry discussions. He also highlighted a "supercycle" for the government segment, driven by accelerating defense budgets and modernization priorities. He further noted that delayed 2025 government projects provide additional upside.

As a result, Citi expects fiscal 2026 government revenue to rise about 51% year-over-year, roughly 8 percentage points above consensus, with upside potential exceeding 70%. Overall, the firm projects Palantir could deliver 70%–80% revenue growth in 2026.

Citi Expects Strong Q4 Results in February

Radke expects Palantir to deliver strong fourth-quarter results when it reports its Q4 2025 earnings on February 2, 2026. He believes Palantir will continue its strong growth into Q4 after two consecutive quarters of double-digit gains. Radke also said 2026 revenue estimates could be raised, thanks to robust U.S. commercial deals and positive feedback from customers using its AIP platform.

Below is a screenshot showing how Palantir's remaining performance obligations (RPO) have grown over the past few quarters, highlighting the company's expanding backlog of contracted revenue. This growth reflects strong demand for Palantir's enterprise and government AI solutions.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: 3 Best Value Stocks with Over 30% Upside in 2026, According to Analysts
data.description:
Investors looking for undervalued stocks in the current volatile market may want to pay close attention to Wall Street's latest picks. These stocks stand out for their solid fundamentals, attractive valuations, and catalysts that could drive meaningful gains in the months ahead.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Before we go further, let's define value stocks. These are companies that look undervalued compared to their true worth, trading at lower price-to-earnings or price-to-book ratios. When the market eventually recognizes their real value, they can deliver strong gains.

Now, let's check the three value stocks that analysts have spotlighted. Each carries a "Strong Buy" consensus rating but also offers more than 30% upside potential from current levels.

Here Are This Week's Stocks

Encompass Health (EHC) – The company provides inpatient rehabilitation services through its nationwide network of rehabilitation hospitals. It has a Strong Buy analyst consensus rating and an average price target of $143.33, implying a 39.48% upside potential from the current levels. The company's P/E of 19.45x reflects a 29.7% discount to the Healthcare sector's median of 27.7.

Last month, Encompass Health reaffirmed FY25 revenue guidance of between $5.905 billion and $5.955 billion, and EPS in the range of $5.22 to $5.37

Smithfield Foods (SFD) – This global packaged meats and fresh pork producer is best known for its pork products and vertically integrated supply chain. Its average price target of $29.75 implies a 36.97% upside potential from the current levels. SFD stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating. Trading at a P/E of 9.8x, the company is valued 53.6% below the Consumer Defensive sector's median multiple of 21.13.

The company raised its fiscal 2025 profit outlook by $25 million, citing strong year‑to‑date results and growing confidence in its strategy.

SkyWest (SKYW) – SkyWest operates a regional airline in the United States. It has a Strong Buy analyst consensus rating and an average price target of $131.00, implying nearly 30% upside potential from the current levels. With a P/E ratio of 9.7x, the stock is priced at a 62.1% discount to the Industrials sector's median of 25.61.

Last month, Citi's John Godyn initiated coverage on SkyWest stock at Hold with a $112 target, saying an "elongated mid‑cycle" from 2026 favors large supermajor airlines over legacy low‑cost carriers.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/13/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Slip Ahead of CPI Data, Big Bank Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down early Tuesday as traders anticipated key Consumer Price Index (CPI) data and big bank results for Q4. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.23%, 0.14%, and 0.12%, respectively, at 3:45 a.m. EST on January 13.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Monday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed in the green, with the S&P 500 rising 0.16%, the Dow gaining 0.17%, and the Nasdaq Composite advancing 0.26%.

Looking ahead, investors will closely watch today's CPI data to gain greater understanding of inflationary trends and fill gaps from last fall's extended U.S. government shutdown. Dow Jones consensus estimates anticipate a 2.7% price rise over the twelve months through December. This figure aligns with November's lower-than-expected CPI results.

Moreover, banking giant JPMorgan (JPM) will kick off the Q4 earnings season today, joined by airline leader Delta Air Lines (DAL).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.19%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $59.82 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,589 per ounce on Tuesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed amid persistent geopolitical tensions involving Iran and Greenland. U.S. President Donald Trump stated last night that any country doing business with Iran will face a 25% tariff on all their business with the U.S.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on January 13 amid Iran's ongoing geopolitical unrest.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 0.90%. In China, the Shanghai Composite fell 0.64%, while the Shenzhen Component added 1.44%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei surged 3.10%, and the Topix added 2.41%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: What Is Microsoft's (MSFT) New Warning on China's AI Push?
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) has warned that Chinese AI companies are gaining users faster than U.S. rivals outside North America and Europe, pointing to rising pressure in the global AI race.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In an interview with the Financial Times, Microsoft President Brad Smith said the fight for AI adoption is now driven by lower costs and wider access to AI tools. That dynamic favors Chinese firms in emerging markets, where low-cost and open AI tools are easier to adopt.

Why Chinese AI Is Gaining Ground Globally

Smith said Chinese AI companies benefit from state backing and open models that allow them to compete strongly on price. He noted that China now has open-source systems that are "competitive," making it easier for Chinese firms to win users in cost-sensitive regions.

Microsoft's research showed that this trend is already visible across parts of Africa, Eastern Europe, and other emerging markets. Chinese AI start-up DeepSeek has seen strong uptake in several countries, helped by lower costs and simple setup, especially where access to U.S. technology is limited.

A Growing AI Divide Raises Concerns

Smith warned that the gap in AI adoption is becoming a "cause for concern." Microsoft's data showed that AI use remains much higher in developed countries than in the global south, increasing the risk of a wider economic gap between regions.

He added that while U.S. companies still hold key strengths, including trust and access to better chips, those advantages alone will not be enough. "You always have to compete on price," Smith said, stressing the need to stay active in fast-growing regions.

Why Global Reach Matters for Microsoft

While Microsoft leads in enterprise software and advanced AI tools, much of the next wave of AI users will come from outside the West, where affordability matters most. Smith cautioned that if U.S. tech firms pull back from these markets, cheaper platforms could define how AI is used going forward.

In short, the next phase of the AI race may depend less on who builds the best model and more on who reaches the most users.

Is Microsoft Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 14% rally in its share price over the past year, the average Microsoft price target of $631.36 per share implies 32.3% upside risk.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Orsted Shares Jump after U.S. Court Lifts Trump-Era Block on Key Wind Project
data.description:
Orsted (DOGEF) shares surged after a major U.S. court ruling cleared the Danish renewable energy giant to resume work on its nearly completed Revolution Wind project. The news lifted investor sentiment as it removed the threat of massive financial losses for the company.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The project, which is roughly 87–90% complete, had been halted twice in recent months. This created uncertainty for Orsted and its partner Skyborn Renewables, which have already invested billions in the development.

Shares of the company were up about 6% in early European trading.

Court Ruling Clears the Way for Orsted

A U.S. District Court judge ruled that construction on the $5 billion project could restart immediately, rejecting the Trump administration's attempt to pause offshore wind development over what it described as national‑security concerns.

The judge said the government's justification appeared insufficient and raised doubt about its real motives.

Orsted said it plans to restart construction "as soon as possible," emphasizing that the project will deliver affordable, reliable power to roughly 350,000 homes across Rhode Island and Connecticut once operational.

Ruling Lifts Outlook for Orsted's U.S. Projects amid Ongoing Policy Clash

The ruling also boosts sentiment about Orsted's broader U.S. portfolio. The decision raises hope for similar outcomes for other halted projects, including the company's larger Sunrise Wind development near New York, which is also tied up in legal challenges.

It is worth noting that the case is part of a wider clash between offshore wind developers and the Trump administration, which suspended five major wind leases late last year, citing Pentagon concerns about turbine interference with radar systems.

Importantly, courts have now twice rejected those arguments in Orsted's case, calling them insufficient to justify stopping work.

Are Orsted Shares a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, DOGEF has a Hold consensus rating based on three Buys, seven Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $21.62, the average Orsted price target implies 8.1% upside potential. Shares of the company have declined 16.85% over the past six months.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
